lines.t: It was almost noon, and the jungle was very still, very silent. Heat waves shimmered along the railway embankment where it cut a path through the tall evergreen trees.
Suraj stood near the cutting, waiting for the midday train. He was not catching a train — he simply wanted to watch the steam engine come roaring out of the tunnel.

The steam engine shot out of the tunnel, snorting and puffing like some green, black, and gold dragon, some beautiful monster out of Suraj's dreams. Showering sparks left and right, it roared a challenge to the jungle.
Suraj turned from his contemplation of the drifting smoke and began walking along the embankment towards the tunnel. The tunnel grew darker as he walked further into it.
When he had gone about twenty yards, it became pitch dark. The walls were damp and sticky. A bat flew past. A lizard scuttled between the lines.
Coming straight from the darkness into the light, Suraj was dazzled by the sudden glare. He looked up at the tree-covered hillside and thought he saw something moving between the trees.
It was just a flash of orange and gold, and a long swishing tail. It was there between the trees for a second or two, and then it was gone.
About fifteen metres from the entrance to the tunnel stood the watchman's hut. Marigolds grew in front of the hut, and at the back there was a small vegetable patch.
Welcome, welcome. I don't often have visitors. Sit down for a while, and tell me why you were inspecting my tunnel. It is my tunnel — I have only lent it to the Government.
It was a leopard you saw, said Sunder Singh. My leopard. It comes to this range for a few days every month. It minds its own business.
There is one train during the day, and there is one train during the night. About nine o'clock, if it isn't late. You could come and sit here with me, if you like.
Everyone goes to sleep in the afternoon, complained Suraj. My father lies down as soon as he has had his lunch. Well, said Sunder Singh, the animals also rest in the heat of the day.

vocab.shimmered: Gave off a soft, wavering light
vocab.embankment: A raised bank of earth built to carry a road or railway
vocab.evergreen: Keeping leaves all year; never bare
vocab.dazzled: Temporarily blinded by sudden bright light
vocab.leopard: A large wild cat with spotted fur, found in Asia and Africa
